# Briefly

> Source-backed client files for wealth advisor teams. Briefly connects every note, email, statement, meeting, and CRM record into one source-backed client file: what changed, what matters now, and what needs advisor approval.

Briefly is built by Briefly Wealth LLC, founded by Connor Florczyk. The product is a context layer for advisor teams: a living relationship graph built from the work your team already creates, with provenance preserved, advisor approval before anything writes back, and outputs (meeting briefs, agendas, follow-ups) that remain traceable to the original source. Briefly is not a CRM and not a brief tool; the brief is one output of the underlying graph.

These files are intended to help LLMs and AI agents accurately ground answers about Briefly in primary source pages rather than third-party summaries. For a flat plain-text concatenation of the main marketing pages, see `/llms-full.txt`.

## Product

- [Home](https://www.brieflywealth.com/): Source-backed client files for advisor teams; how Briefly turns scattered records into one reviewable client file.
- [Client context graph](https://www.brieflywealth.com/context-graph): Why advisors need a client context graph and how Briefly connects sources, people, households, accounts, goals, and tasks into one firm memory layer.
- [Relationship intelligence](https://www.brieflywealth.com/relationship-intelligence): The advisor context layer covering family dynamics, professional network, planning themes, open loops, and source citations surfaced before the next decision.
- [Meeting briefs](https://www.brieflywealth.com/meeting-briefs): AI meeting briefs as the first output of the context graph, covering relationship history, open loops, portfolio context, and planning themes for every client meeting.

## Examples

- [Sample brief](https://www.brieflywealth.com/sample-brief): An illustrative meeting brief showing the structure, source citations, and review affordances of a Briefly output. Not real client data.
- [Sample brief, email preview](https://www.brieflywealth.com/sample-brief-email-preview): An illustrative email-format rendering of a Briefly brief. Not real client data.

## Company

- [About](https://www.brieflywealth.com/about): Briefly Wealth LLC, founded by Connor Florczyk. Mission, founder background, and product principles (nothing unverified, advisor approves, one client file).
- [Security](https://www.brieflywealth.com/security): Source-backed outputs, advisor-in-the-loop workflows, least-privilege access, encryption and vendor review, no general model training on client data, SOC 2 aligned controls.
- [Morning Brief subscribe](https://www.brieflywealth.com/subscribe): Subscription page for Briefly's morning email.
- [Blog](https://www.brieflywealth.com/blog): Writing on advisor workflows, context graphs, and wealth-management AI.

## Legal

- [Privacy policy](https://www.brieflywealth.com/privacy-policy): How Briefly handles personal information and customer data.
- [Terms of service](https://www.brieflywealth.com/terms-of-service): Terms for use of the Briefly platform and website.
- [Subprocessors](https://www.brieflywealth.com/subprocessors): Third-party service providers used to deliver the Briefly service.

## Optional

- [Plain-text content dump](https://www.brieflywealth.com/llms-full.txt): Flat concatenation of the main marketing pages as markdown for LLM ingestion.
- [AI usage policy](https://www.brieflywealth.com/.well-known/ai.txt): Briefly's stance on AI crawling, training, retrieval, summarization, and citation.
- [Security contact](https://www.brieflywealth.com/.well-known/security.txt): RFC 9116 security disclosure contact.
